rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ELLIOTT H WHEELER TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues may include: 1) Petitioners request authority to distribute assets in the manner called for by the trust. It is unclear why court approval is needed. 2) Petitioners request authority to withdraw funds from a blocked account for distribution to charitable beneficiaries, but no charities are named and no amounts given. 3) Petitioners request authority to hire an academic consultant to assist the Trustee. No rate is provided and allegations are not made as to whether the consultant is related to any of the parties here or otherwise have any interest and why court authority is needed. 4) Petitioners request authority to withdraw from blocked account for ongoing expenses not to exceed $150k per year. Bond is not requested to be increased. Remote appearances are highly encouraged. Please review the 6th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 6th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SMALLEY FAMILY TRUST (1992 & AS AMENDED)
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/9/20. The "Declaration" filed on 9/21/20 only partially addressed the many issues set forth in the Examiner's Notes dated 7/30/20. Still no proof of service of notice of the amendment filed on 6/27/19 has been filed. The account appears to be missing an informational schedule. Credit card statements (redacted for personal information) have not been filed for some of the purchases made in June and November 2016. There were other questions regarding what appear to be duplicate (triplicate?) payments to Adriana's House Cleaning in 2017 and an $8,000 payment to Bornstein Law, as well as requests for clarification regarding some of the trustee's time entries. The "Declaration" filed on 9/21/20 only addressed, incompletely, the trustee's attorney's time entries and costs not set forth in detail. Questions about Ms. Canny's time entries and costs not set forth in detail have not yet been addressed. Although the "Declaration" claims that the trustee's attorney's time was justified in amending the third account, which was filed in its incomplete form in November 2018 just to meet the filing deadline, the amendments that included the full disclosure of fees being requested were not filed until June 2019 after numerous continuances at the request of the attorney. If courtesy copies of responsive pleadings and the proof of service of the notice of the amendment filed on 6/27/19 are not received by the Examiner by 10/28/20, the hearing will be continued again.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ANDREW R. DOEDEN MINOR'S SETTLEMENT TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Directing Establishment Of Minor'S Settlement Trust, Authorizing Investment In Mutual Funds And Governement Bonds, And Approving Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Court is to determine whether petitioner has standing to file this petition. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ALVERN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/16/20. Notice may not be mailed in care of another person unless the person entitled to notice is an adult and has directed in writing that notice be mailed in care of the other person. [CRC 7.51(a)(2).] A copy of the petition was not served with the notice. [Probate Code Section 851.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FELDER SHARP GRAHAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/28/20. No courtesy copies. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.93 G.] No proposed order. [LRSF 14.6 A.] No proof of publication filed yet. The petition was not properly signed or verified (not dated). It appears that notice was mailed by the petitioner. A non-resident personal representative will be required to post at least minimum bond, as determined by the information presented in a declaration setting forth the anticipated claims against the estate and estimated liabilities of the decedent. [LRSF 14.37.] When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.26 D. and E.] Courtesy copies of the petition and any supplement to the petition filed to address these issues, as well as the proposed order, must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/19/20 to avoid another continuance.
